texto: Abba is not Hebrew, the language of liturgy, but Aramaic, the language of home and everyday life … We need to be wary of the suggestion … that the correct translation of Abba is ‘Daddy.’ Abba is the intimate word of a family circle where that obedient reverence was at the heart of the relationship, whereas Daddy is the familiar word of a family circle from which all thoughts of reverence and obedience have largely disappeared … The best English translation of Abba is simply ‘Dear Father.
explicacao: Citação de Thomas Allan Smail sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Abba is not Hebrew, the language of liturgy, but Aramaic, the language of home and everyday life …” articula familia, abba, christianity em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como christianity e daddy.
